string.text: Smart Balance keeps each map's population near what the map itself declares. It reads the game's own spawner records: how many squads each spawner is allowed and how many are alive right now, per faction, per map, with all mutants as one group. Wiped-out groups come back sooner and arrive stronger, weakened squads are repaired, overgrown groups wait longer. Corrections avoid already-crowded areas, healthy groups are left completely alone, and the game engine still decides every spawn.
Master toggle. When off, nothing is measured and nothing is corrected.

How many off-screen NPCs an area must hold before it counts as crowded. Crowded areas receive no accelerated respawns and no squad repairs, and their spawners are delayed, so recovery flows toward the empty parts of a map instead of feeding existing piles. Lower values spread the population harder; higher values allow denser hotspots.

Respawn Pacing advances or delays each spawner's cooldown in proportion to how far its faction is from the map's declared squad count. A wiped faction returns at full speed, a slightly depleted one gets a nudge, an overgrown one idles. Spawns are never blocked and never created directly; only the timing of the game's own respawns moves, inside the limits below.
Toggle for the cooldown corrections. When off, verdicts are still measured (Spawn Size and Squad Refill keep using them) but no cooldown is touched.

How many measurement passes carry one spawner from full cooldown to the floor at maximum depletion (a pass covers all tracked squads, several minutes of real time). At 1 a single pass applies the full correction; higher values spread it out. Partial depletion scales each push down proportionally. Delays use the same step in the opposite direction.

The cooldown that must always remain after an acceleration, in game-minutes. The engine ages the final wait out on its own clock and fires the respawn from there. A spawner already within this margin of firing naturally is skipped. Raise to leave more delay between depletion and refill; lower for snappier turnover.

The ceiling on remaining cooldown after a delay, in game-minutes. Delays stop at this ceiling even when the installed respawn cooldown is longer; one full vanilla cooldown stays the bound when it is shorter. Raise to idle overgrown factions longer; lower to keep recovery moving everywhere.

Spawn Size makes new squads of a faction that is losing across the whole Zone arrive with more members, up to their own configured maximum. A faction merely dented on one map spawns normal squads; Zone-wide collapse brings reinforcements at strength. Never beyond a squad's own range, never on a map where that faction is overgrown, never into a crowded area.
Toggle for the spawn-strength response. When off, every squad spawns at its vanilla random size.
Scales how strongly Zone-wide depletion raises new squad sizes. At 100 a fully collapsed faction spawns squads at their configured maximum; at 50 halfway up their range; at 0 the response is off.

Squad Refill repairs squads that lost members and fell below their own minimum size. Nothing in the game restores a weakened squad, and a lone survivor blocks its spawner's slot forever. The refill returns one member per pass, only while the squad is off-screen, only up to the squad's own configured minimum, and never in crowded areas. Fixed-roster and story squads are untouched.
Toggle for the squad repairs. When off, weakened squads stay at whatever size combat left them.

Diagnostic tools for tuning and troubleshooting. Raise log level for structured tracing of every pass, verdict, advance, delay, refill, and spawn-size raise to alifebalance.log (small performance cost at DEBUG).

Controls verbosity of alifebalance.log. ERROR logs crashes only. WARN adds unexpected conditions. INFO adds startup, configuration, and reset events. DEBUG traces every pass, verdict, correction, refill, and spawn-size raise. Default WARN is silent in normal play; DEBUG is for tuning or bug reports.

Drops a green PDA map marker on every smart terrain that receives a cooldown advance or delay. The marker lingers for five real-time minutes and then clears itself. Right-click any marker to teleport to that smart or display its full correction history. Independent of log level.

Restore all AlifeBalance MCM settings to factory defaults. Use after upgrading from a version with different MCM structure (the old settings remain as orphaned keys in the MCM storage file but no longer apply).
Warning: save or discard pending MCM changes first; otherwise they override the reset on the next Accept.
